prompts.text: Explain to a scared patient what sepsis means and why we're admitting them.
Explain to a worried mom what an NSTEMI is.
Explain to a patient's daughter what hospice actually is.
Explain to a new admit what palliative care covers and doesn't.
Explain to a teenager why their dad is on a ventilator.

Explain to a worried spouse what a stroke code means in plain words.
Explain to a discharge patient what afib is and why warfarin matters.
Explain to a family what brain death means before the apnea test.
Tell the story of the last patient whose family really got what was happening — what made the explanation land?
Walk a new resident through how you'd narrate a code blue to the patient's wife after — in a way she'd remember tomorrow.

Mid-rounds the attending asks: 'How would you explain why we're holding pressors?' Build an analogy without preamble.
